ancien-remparts-chateau-lullin-lullin.name: Former Ramparts of the Château de Lullin
ancien-remparts-chateau-lullin-lullin.meta_title: Former ramparts of the Château de Lullin · Lullin (74) | Loisirs 74
ancien-remparts-chateau-lullin-lullin.meta_description: Remains of the 14th-century Château de Lullin: an enclosure wall against the presbytery with arrow slits, the last witness of the Faucigny fortress in the Haute-Savoie pre-Alps.
ancien-remparts-chateau-lullin-lullin.hero_alt: Former enclosure wall of the Château de Lullin with arrow slits, a medieval remnant visible in the village
ancien-remparts-chateau-lullin-lullin.body.what_is:
The former ramparts of the Château de Lullin are the last surviving traces of a major medieval fortress built in 1304 by Hugues du Faucigny on a natural mound that overlooks the Follaz valley by 65 metres, in the village of Lullin (74470), halfway between the Chablais and the Faucigny. Allied with the Count of Geneva against the House of Savoy, the Dauphin of Faucigny fortified the site: 300 metres of ramparts were raised or renovated by 892 workers, turning the village into a fully walled town.

The threat was taken seriously by Amadeus V of Savoy, who sent his son Edward to besiege the fortress. After eleven days of siege in 1305, Lullin fell to the Savoyards, who in turn built permanent stone fortifications. The castle continued to grow until the 16th century, reaching imposing dimensions — 92 metres long by 45 wide — before being destroyed by a fire in 1564. Quickly falling into ruin, it was gradually dismantled and the stone reused for the parish church and several village houses.

Today only a single fragment of the enclosure wall survives, leaning against the back of the presbytery in the village centre. Pierced with arrow slits and built of rough-cut stone, this short stretch of wall is the only physical reminder of the medieval fortress of Lullin and of the dramatic episode in Faucigny–Savoy relations that preceded the final union of the region under Savoy.

balade-pedestre-tour-lac-mole-tour.name: Lake of Le Môle Loop Walk
balade-pedestre-tour-lac-mole-tour.meta_title: Lake of Le Môle Loop · La Tour (74) · Accessible loop ≈ 1.6 km
balade-pedestre-tour-lac-mole-tour.meta_description: Family-friendly accessible loop around the lake of Le Môle at La Tour (74): ≈ 1.6 km, 21 m of climb, woodland and lake at the foot of Le Môle (1,863 m).
balade-pedestre-tour-lac-mole-tour.hero_alt: Lakeside path around the lake of Le Môle at La Tour in Haute-Savoie, accessible family loop
balade-pedestre-tour-lac-mole-tour.body.what_is:
The Lake of Le Môle Loop walk is a very short and easy family loop around the lake of Le Môle at La Tour (74250), a small Faucigny village adjoining the neighbouring village of Saint-Jeoire. The trail is about 1.6 km long with only 21 metres of cumulative ascent and tops out at 613 m altitude — more a stroll than a real hike. The setting: a quiet body of water, meadows, shaded woodland and the silhouette of Le Môle (1,863 m), the emblematic peak of the Faucigny, rising above the valley.

The walk starts at the entrance to Saint-Jeoire village, at the foot of the Château de La Tour, along a small path (the Chaffards path) that runs slightly above the local road. You reach the lake of Le Môle, complete a full loop around it, then drop back down through the village of La Tour before rejoining the forest track that leads back to the starting oasis. On the wildlife side, many birds nest around the lake, making it a particularly rich spot for observation in spring.

The loop's main feature is that it is accessible by wheelchair and stroller: the path surface is even, the gradients are very gentle and the length suits all family configurations. It is also a pleasant choice during heatwaves, with shaded passages around the lake, or after the schools restart when the surrounding hikes empty out.

base-nautique-evian-bains.name: Évian-les-Bains Watersports Centre
base-nautique-evian-bains.meta_title: Évian-les-Bains Watersports Centre · Paddle · Kayak · Sailing
base-nautique-evian-bains.meta_description: Évian-les-Bains watersports centre: paddle, kayak, sailing, rowing and diving sessions on Lake Geneva, supervised beach June–September. Bookable evening sessions.
base-nautique-evian-bains.hero_alt: Évian-les-Bains watersports centre on Lake Geneva: paddle, kayak, sailing, diving and rowing to explore the southern shore
base-nautique-evian-bains.body.what_is:
The Évian-les-Bains watersports centre is one of the main access points to water activities on the southern shore of Lake Geneva. Set on the Évian beach area, on avenue du Général Dupas, it offers both self-guided rentals (paddle, single or double kayak, pedalo, rowing boats) and supervised sessions run by the instructors of Évian's historic sailing club.

Practical info: the centre is open every day in summer from 1 pm to 7 pm, with a late session on Thursday until 9 pm. Supervised sessions run all summer: kayak and stand-up paddle on Wednesday and Saturday evenings, rowing on Monday/Wednesday/Friday afternoons, sailing on Wednesday afternoon from early July to late August. A supervised swimming area is also open on the adjacent beach from June to September (12 pm–6 pm, 11 am–7 pm in July–August).

The centre lets visitors and locals alike enjoy Lake Geneva in a unique setting — the Léman opening onto the Swiss shore, Évian's belle-époque promenade, the panorama of the Vaud mountains — at affordable group rates (paddle from €15/h, kayak €20/h, supervised sessions around €25/session).

au-fil-rail-jeu-piste-a-servoz-servoz.name: Au Fil du Rail Treasure Hunt at Servoz
au-fil-rail-jeu-piste-a-servoz-servoz.meta_title: Au Fil du Rail Treasure Hunt at Servoz · Free · OT Chamonix
au-fil-rail-jeu-piste-a-servoz-servoz.meta_description: Free family treasure hunt at Servoz (Chamonix valley): ~2 h walk from the SNCF station, question booklet picked up at the Chamonix Valley Tourist Offices.
au-fil-rail-jeu-piste-a-servoz-servoz.hero_alt: Au Fil du Rail treasure hunt at Servoz in the Chamonix valley, free family trail around the village
au-fil-rail-jeu-piste-a-servoz-servoz.body.what_is:
The Au Fil du Rail treasure hunt at Servoz is a fully free family discovery trail, designed by the Chamonix-Mont-Blanc Valley Tourist Office to introduce visitors to the village and its authentic mountain-town spirit. The hunt is part of a collection of four valley trails (Servoz, Vallorcine, Les Bois de Chamonix, Argentière) linked by the common theme of the Mont-Blanc Express railway, the emblematic line that serves all these villages from Saint-Gervais up to the Swiss border.

The route starts at Servoz SNCF station and invites young and old to decipher the village for about two hours, answering questions whose clues are hidden in the landscapes, buildings, heritage and signage of the town. Servoz, a former mining commune set at 815 m altitude at the foot of Mont Blanc, is the perfect playground: Maison de l'Alpage, Maison du Lieutenant, Diosaz Gorges, Vaudagne bridge, plus the flower meadows in summer. The pace is gentle, with no physical difficulty, ideal for children from around 6 onwards.

To take part, simply pick up the question booklet free of charge at any of the Tourist Offices in the Chamonix-Mont-Blanc Valley (Servoz, Chamonix, Argentière). The trail can be done at any time of year, although spring and summer offer the most pleasant conditions. Once back at the start, walkers can have their booklet checked and pick up a small souvenir.

au-fil-rail-jeu-piste-a-vallorcine-vallo.name: Au Fil du Rail Treasure Hunt at Vallorcine
au-fil-rail-jeu-piste-a-vallorcine-vallo.meta_title: Au Fil du Rail Treasure Hunt at Vallorcine · 3 km · Free
au-fil-rail-jeu-piste-a-vallorcine-vallo.meta_description: Free family treasure hunt at Vallorcine (Chamonix valley): 3 km, ~1 h 30, starts at the Buet SNCF station, question booklet from the valley Tourist Offices.
au-fil-rail-jeu-piste-a-vallorcine-vallo.hero_alt: Au Fil du Rail treasure hunt at Vallorcine in the Chamonix valley, 3 km family trail around the village
au-fil-rail-jeu-piste-a-vallorcine-vallo.body.what_is:
The Au Fil du Rail treasure hunt at Vallorcine is a free family discovery trail of 3 kilometres and about 1 h 30 of walking, created by the Chamonix-Mont-Blanc Valley Tourist Office. The hunt is part of a collection of four "Au Fil du Rail" trails (Servoz, Vallorcine, Les Bois de Chamonix, Argentière) linked by the theme of the Mont-Blanc Express, the iconic TER line that links Saint-Gervais to Vallorcine and continues on the Swiss side towards Martigny.

The route starts at the Buet SNCF station in Vallorcine, the last French village before the Swiss border, nestled at 1,260 m altitude at the foot of the Aiguilles Rouges range and Mont Blanc. The question booklet invites walkers to explore the Buet hamlet, the village of Vallorcine and its mountain heritage: wooden chalets, Notre-Dame de l'Assomption church, the old school, sites evoking mountain culture and mining. Answers are hidden in the landscape and signage: observe, look, learn while walking.

To take part, simply pick up the question booklet free of charge at any of the valley Tourist Offices (Vallorcine, Argentière, Chamonix). The trail is doable year-round, though spring through autumn is the most comfortable window. The format suits families with children aged 6 and up.

borne-chateau-vuache-vulbens.name: Vuache Castle Boundary Stone
borne-chateau-vuache-vulbens.meta_title: Vuache Castle Boundary Stone · Vulbens (74) · 1579 remnant
borne-chateau-vuache-vulbens.meta_description: Last remnant of the Vuache seigneurial castle at Vulbens: a stone marker dated 1579 at the foot of the church steps — freely accessible year-round.
borne-chateau-vuache-vulbens.hero_alt: Stone marker dated 1579, last remnant of the Vuache castle at Vulbens, at the foot of the church steps, Haute-Savoie
borne-chateau-vuache-vulbens.body.what_is:
The Vuache Castle boundary stone is a commemorative stone dated 1579, sealed at the foot of the steps near the church of Vulbens (74520). Discreet but history-rich, it is today the only tangible remnant of the seigneurial castle of Vuache, which once overlooked the village from a rise about fifteen metres above the current church.

The castle, built in the early 14th century when the region belonged to the County of Geneva, played a triple role: military fortress, temporary residence of the lords of Vuache, and administrative centre of the mandement (seigneurial territory). It was progressively abandoned in the mid-17th century, when the seigneury's seat was moved to the nearby hamlet of Faramaz. The stones were reused in village constructions and the castle quickly vanished from the landscape.

For the history-curious visitor, the 1579 stone is a discreet but evocative starting point: read the engraved date, imagine the silhouette of the castle perched above the church, enjoy the panorama over the Genevois plain and the Vuache range. Free year-round visit.

boucle-pedestre-detective-nature-jonzier.name: Détective Nature Walking Loop (Vuache Nature Trail)
boucle-pedestre-detective-nature-jonzier.meta_title: Détective Nature Loop · Vuache Nature Trail · Jonzier-Épagny
boucle-pedestre-detective-nature-jonzier.meta_description: Free family loop walk at Jonzier-Épagny (74): Vuache Nature Trail with 15 interpretive panels + free Détective Nature game booklets for ages 8–10 and 10+.
boucle-pedestre-detective-nature-jonzier.hero_alt: Détective Nature family loop on the Vuache Nature Trail at Jonzier-Épagny, with interpretive panels in Haute-Savoie
boucle-pedestre-detective-nature-jonzier.body.what_is:
The Détective Nature walking loop is a family-friendly educational circuit on the Vuache Nature Trail, a discovery path created by the Vuache Inter-municipal Authority (SIV) across the villages of Jonzier-Épagny, Chênex, Dingy-en-Vuache and Savigny, just 15 km from Geneva. The idea: turn a walk into a treasure hunt, supported by the network of 15 educational panels along the trail.

The SIV provides two free Détective Nature booklets, downloadable from pays-du-vuache.fr or available at local Tourist Offices and the town hall — one for ages 8–10, one for ages 10 and up. Each booklet contains a question-and-answer game whose answers can be found on the trail panels (fauna, flora, geology, habitat), plus crosswords, word searches, animals to colour or draw.

The format suits families with primary-school children, school outings and nature clubs. The trail is doable in any season but spring and early summer are richest for wildlife observation.

boucles-coteau-publier.name: The Coteau Loops
boucles-coteau-publier.meta_description: Network of walking trails at Publier (74): three concentric green/blue/red loops between the village and the Amphion plain, 5.5 km / 200 m climb, views of Lake Geneva.
boucles-coteau-publier.hero_alt: Coteau Loops walking network at Publier in Haute-Savoie, three concentric trails with views of Lake Geneva
boucles-coteau-publier.body.what_is:
The Coteau Loops form a network of shaded woodland walking trails crisscrossing the commune of Publier (74500), between its hillside village and the Amphion-les-Bains plain on Lake Geneva. The route combines mixed forest, orchards, meadows and balcony viewpoints over Lake Geneva and the Swiss shore (Lausanne, Vevey).

The network is organised in three concentric loops marked with the classic hiking colour code: green (easiest), blue (intermediate), red (longest). About 5.5 km of trails and 200 m of cumulative climb total, averaging 2 hours at a family pace; shorter loops complete in 1 hour. Five entry panels are installed around the commune as gateways to the network, with maps and difficulty ratings.

The Coteau Loops are a good option for half-day families looking for fresh air between the lake and the foothills, with shaded passages in summer and lake panoramas in clear weather.

bourg-et-ruines-chateau-chaumont-chaumon.name: Chaumont Village and Castle Ruins
bourg-et-ruines-chateau-chaumont-chaumon.meta_title: Chaumont village and castle ruins · Chaumont (74270)
bourg-et-ruines-chateau-chaumont-chaumon.meta_description: Medieval ruins of the Château de Chaumont (former châtellenie of the counts of Geneva, razed c. 1630): guided tours of the village by the Heritage Guides.
bourg-et-ruines-chateau-chaumont-chaumon.hero_alt: Ruins of the medieval Château de Chaumont and historic village above the Rhône valley in Haute-Savoie
bourg-et-ruines-chateau-chaumont-chaumon.body.what_is:
The Château de Chaumont, whose romantic ruins still crown a rocky spur above the Rhône, was one of the strongholds of the County of Geneva. Until the early 15th century it housed a châtellenie — an administrative and military district — controlling a vast territory in the south-west Genevois. The sale of the County of Geneva to the Count of Savoy in 1401 was a decisive blow: stripped of its strategic role, the fortress was no longer maintained.

The killing blow came around 1630, during the War of the Mantuan Succession: the royal troops of Louis XIII attacked and razed the castle. Since then the ruins have crumbled, but the site retains a strong atmosphere, perched above the village, with a wide panorama over the Rhône valley, the Vuache range and (on clear days) the Jura.

The Heritage Guides of the Pays d'art et d'histoire run themed walks ("Castles and lords of the Genevois") that include Chaumont alongside other châtellenies. Free walk around the village; the ruins themselves are visible from marked paths.

casino-saint-julien-saint-julien-en-gene.name: Saint-Julien Casino
casino-saint-julien-saint-julien-en-gene.meta_description: Saint-Julien-en-Genevois casino at the gates of Geneva: 112 slot machines, blackjack, roulette, poker, 3 restaurants. Open daily from 10 am.
casino-saint-julien-saint-julien-en-gene.hero_alt: Casino de Saint-Julien-en-Genevois near Geneva: slot machines, table games, bars and restaurants
casino-saint-julien-saint-julien-en-gene.body.what_is:
The Casino de Saint-Julien-en-Genevois is a gaming establishment on the route d'Annecy, at the gates of Geneva. Its border location makes it one of the most accessible casinos from the Geneva metro area — less than 10 minutes by car. The venue combines slot floor, table-game lounge, poker room and three restaurants and bars, making it a full evening out, not only a destination for players.

The slot floor has 112 machines: 11 video pokers, 13 classic reels and 88 video reels from international brands. Slot hours: Sunday–Thursday 10 am–2 am, Friday–Saturday 10 am–3 am. The table-game lounge opens every evening from 8 pm: 2 blackjack tables, 3 traditional English roulettes, 4 Texas Hold'em poker tables and 15 electronic English roulettes.

Three on-site restaurants (gastronomic, brasserie and bar) complete the offer. Free parking. ID required (over 18, no exclusion order).

chateau-buffavent-lully.name: Château de Buffavent
chateau-buffavent-lully.meta_title: Château de Buffavent · Lully (74) · MH 1944 · External view
chateau-buffavent-lully.meta_description: 15th-century fortified manor listed MH 1944 at Lully (Chablais): four round corner towers with conical roofs, taken by the Genevans in 1590. Visible from outside.
chateau-buffavent-lully.hero_alt: Château de Buffavent at Lully in the Chablais: 15th-century fortified manor with four round corner towers
chateau-buffavent-lully.body.what_is:
The Château de Buffavent is a 15th-century fortified manor set on a natural terrace about 800 metres north-west of the village of Lully (74890), in the Chablais. Its evocative name reportedly comes from "bouffâ" — to blow in Savoyard dialect — pointing to a wind-exposed site. The trapezoidal main building is about 18.6 m long by 15.8 m wide, with four round corner towers crowned by conical "pepper-pot" roofs, characteristic of 15th-century Savoyard military architecture.

The castle was built by the lords of Langin as a fortified hunting residence, during the expansion phase that peaked in the 15th–16th centuries. In 1590, Genevan soldiers attacked the castle during the wars between Savoy and Genevan Protestantism: the arrow slits of the left tower still bear traces of cannon fire.

The château is private property, not open to the public, but is easily visible from the surrounding paths around Lully. Listed as a Monument Historique in 1944.

chateau-comtal-saint-julien-en-genevois.name: Château Comtal de Ternier
chateau-comtal-saint-julien-en-genevois.meta_description: Medieval remains of the Comtal de Ternier castle at Saint-Julien-en-Genevois: seat of the châtellenie ceded to the Counts of Geneva, separated from La Poype by a defensive ditch.
chateau-comtal-saint-julien-en-genevois.hero_alt: Remains of the Château Comtal de Ternier on the hill above Saint-Julien-en-Genevois in Haute-Savoie
chateau-comtal-saint-julien-en-genevois.body.what_is:
The Château Comtal de Ternier is one of the two medieval remnants crowning the hill of Ternier, a hamlet of the commune of Saint-Julien-en-Genevois (74160), about two kilometres south of the town centre. Built in the 13th century, it was the seat of the Châtellenie de Ternier, an important seigneury controlling the territories south of Geneva, between the Arve and the Laire.

The fortress originally belonged to the Ternier family, an influential lineage that sometimes rivalled the Counts of Geneva. The castle was then ceded to the Counts and became a comital residence and administrative seat — hence the name "Comtal". Not far away stood another fortification, La Poype, owned by the Ternier family proper: the two castles were separated by a mound and two defensive ditches still readable in the landscape.

The site is accessible by marked paths from the Ternier hamlet. Walls and earthworks visible; free access.

chateau-croix-scionzier.name: Château de la Croix
chateau-croix-scionzier.meta_title: Château de la Croix · Scionzier (74) · MH 1994 · External view
chateau-croix-scionzier.meta_description: 16th-century fortified manor listed MH 1994 at Scionzier (Faucigny): 17th-century wall paintings and 1840 panoramic wallpaper. Visible from the road, closed to the public.
chateau-croix-scionzier.hero_alt: Château de la Croix at Scionzier, 16th-century fortified manor overlooking the Arve valley in the Faucigny
chateau-croix-scionzier.body.what_is:
The Château de la Croix is a historic fortified manor that overlooks the Arve valley from the commune of Scionzier (74950), a few kilometres downstream from Cluses, in the Faucigny. While local tradition mentions a medieval 12th-century core, the building as it stands today is essentially a 16th-century construction, built by the lords of La Croix, who later allied by marriage with the Rochette family. The castle's history is closely tied to that of the Faucigny and its noble lines.

The château preserves several remarkable decorative features: 17th-century wall paintings — exceptional in the region — and notably an 1840 panoramic wallpaper in one of the salons, a rare piece of 19th-century classical interior décor. Several parts of the castle were listed as Monument Historique in 1988, then more broadly in 1994.

The château is privately owned and not open to the public, but is clearly visible from the road through Scionzier.

chateau-fonbonne-et-herbularius-evian-ba.name: Château de Fonbonne and its Herbularius
chateau-fonbonne-et-herbularius-evian-ba.meta_title: Château de Fonbonne and Herbularius · Évian-les-Bains (74)
chateau-fonbonne-et-herbularius-evian-ba.meta_description: 14th-century fortified manor in the heart of Évian-les-Bains with its Herbularius medieval-inspired garden (sage, rosemary, fennel, horseradish), laid out in 2011.
chateau-fonbonne-et-herbularius-evian-ba.hero_alt: Château de Fonbonne and its medieval-inspired Herbularius garden in the heart of Évian-les-Bains
chateau-fonbonne-et-herbularius-evian-ba.body.what_is:
The Château de Fonbonne (sometimes spelled Fontbonne) is one of the discreet but singular heritage buildings at the heart of Évian-les-Bains. A fortified manor stood on this site from the 14th century, helping to defend the new Touvière quarter — the upper town outside Évian's enclosure, created by Amadeus V in 1303. The building was acquired in 1559 by Thomas Jacquerod and passed to the de Loys de Bonnevaux family.

In the late 18th century the wealthy English writer William Beckford stayed there and threw memorable parties. Transformed into a hotel around 1860, the building was reshaped by later restorations and today looks more 19th-century than medieval. Its garden, however, is the main draw.

In 2011 the Évian municipal services laid out the former garden as an Herbularius, a medieval-style herb garden featuring aromatic and medicinal plants (sage, rosemary, fennel, horseradish, lavender, hyssop). Open during city park hours; free access.

chateau-sonnaz-thonon-bains.name: Château de Sonnaz
chateau-sonnaz-thonon-bains.meta_title: Château de Sonnaz · Chablais Museum · Thonon-les-Bains
chateau-sonnaz-thonon-bains.meta_description: Castle built in 1666 by the Gerbaix de Sonnaz in the heart of Thonon-les-Bains, today home to the Chablais Museum and Tourist Office, facing Lake Geneva.
chateau-sonnaz-thonon-bains.hero_alt: Château de Sonnaz at Thonon-les-Bains, home to the Chablais Museum, facing Lake Geneva
chateau-sonnaz-thonon-bains.body.what_is:
The Château de Sonnaz is one of Thonon-les-Bains' emblematic monuments, set at the heart of the upper town facing Lake Geneva, with sweeping views of the Swiss shore and the Jura range. It was built in 1666 by the Gerbaix de Sonnaz, one of the oldest Savoyard noble families, on the ruins of the medieval castle of Thonon built by Marie of Burgundy (wife of Amadeus VIII) and dismantled in the 16th century. The sovereigns of the House of Savoy regularly stayed here during their visits to the Chablais.

The building, sober and harmonious in its classical Grand Siècle style, is today a cultural hub for the Chablais. It houses both the Chablais Museum and the Thonon Tourist Office — a rare combination that makes it a must-visit. The Chablais Museum offers a permanent exhibition on the region's history, daily life and traditional crafts.

Open Tue–Sun (closed Mon and public holidays). Adult admission around €4; free under 18 and on the first Sunday of the month.

chateau-thenieres-ballaison.name: Château de Thénières
chateau-thenieres-ballaison.meta_description: Pseudo-medieval castle built in 1863 by architect Bruger for Ernest de Boigne at 733 m altitude on the Boissy hillside. Now seat of the CCBC.
chateau-thenieres-ballaison.hero_alt: Château de Thénières at Ballaison, 19th-century pseudo-medieval folly with towers above Lake Geneva
chateau-thenieres-ballaison.body.what_is:
The Château de Thénières is one of the most picturesque "fantasy castles" of the Chablais. It was built in 1863 at the request of General Count Ernest de Boigne — heir to the family fortune amassed in India by his great-uncle, the famous General Benoît de Boigne — on the ruins of an earlier medieval fortress. The architect Bruger designed a pseudo-medieval silhouette imitating a fortified castle, with corner towers, crenellations and purely decorative machicolations.

The building stands on the Boissy hillside at 733 m altitude, overlooking the commune of Ballaison (74140) and offering panoramic views of Lake Geneva, the Swiss shore and, in clear weather, the distant Jura. Ernest de Boigne acquired the land in 1811 from the Genevan Jean Lasserre, in line with his family's post-Empire patrimonial strategy.

The castle is now home to the offices of the Communauté de Communes du Bas-Chablais (CCBC). Exterior visible from the public road; interior closed to the public.

chatel-accrobranche-1600-chatel.name: Châtel Accrobranche 1600
chatel-accrobranche-1600-chatel.meta_title: Châtel Accrobranche 1600 · 7 courses · Quick Jump · Summer
chatel-accrobranche-1600-chatel.meta_description: Treetop adventure park at 1,600 m altitude at Châtel (Portes du Soleil): 7 courses from age 2, Quick Jump free fall, top of the Super-Châtel cable car. Summer 2026.
chatel-accrobranche-1600-chatel.hero_alt: Châtel Accrobranche 1600 treetop park at 1,600 m altitude in the Portes du Soleil, near the Super-Châtel cable car
chatel-accrobranche-1600-chatel.body.what_is:
The Châtel Accrobranche 1600 (marketed as Châtel Aventures) is a treetop adventure park set in natural forest at the top of the Super-Châtel cable car, at 1,600 m altitude, in the heart of the Portes du Soleil ski area. Open in summer, it offers 7 all-natural courses exploiting the high-altitude forest: zip lines, monkey bridges, vines, nets, pendulum jumps, all under instructor supervision.

The courses are graded for every age and size: P'tit Kid for children from age 2 and up to 1.05 m (with an adult), 2 Kid courses for 1.05–1.35 m, and 4 Super courses from 1.35 m (or 1.50 m without adult). All jumps, bridges and zip lines use a continuous lifeline system — fully securing progress, a real advantage for younger climbers.

Access via the Super-Châtel cable car (foot passenger ticket included with the activity in many packages). Summer 2026 season, daily during school holidays.

chemin-alpage-parcours-decouverte-habere.name: Path of the Alpage — Discovery Trail
chemin-alpage-parcours-decouverte-habere.meta_title: Path of the Alpage · Habère-Lullin · 6.5 km · 8 panels
chemin-alpage-parcours-decouverte-habere.meta_description: Discovery trail 6.5 km / 330 m climb at Habère-Lullin (Vallée Verte) tracing Guy Chatiliez's book 'Alpage de mon enfance': 8 panels + QR codes.
chemin-alpage-parcours-decouverte-habere.hero_alt: Path of the Alpage discovery trail at Habère-Lullin in the Vallée Verte, mountain pastures and 8 themed panels
chemin-alpage-parcours-decouverte-habere.body.what_is:
The Path of the Alpage is a marked discovery trail inviting walkers to explore the Vallée Verte in the footsteps of Guy Chatiliez, author of the 1977 book Alpage de mon enfance. From a Savoyard family at Habère-Lullin, Chatiliez recounted his childhood memories in the alpine pastures his mother took him to from age six. The trail revisits these places and invites walkers, panel by panel, to relive the atmospheres and gestures of alpine pastoral life described in the book.

The route measures 6.5 km with 330 m of cumulative climb, about 2 h 30 of walking at an easy pace. The start is behind Habère-Lullin church, a 10-minute walk from the village centre, on a gently descending path with dry toilets along the way. The trail features 8 themed stations, each with a panel covering an aspect of alpine pasture life: cheese-making, herd, gestures, equipment, seasons.

QR codes on each panel link to audio extracts from the book. Free year-round, best from May to October.

chemin-art-nature-andilly-andilly.name: Andilly Art-Nature Trail
chemin-art-nature-andilly-andilly.meta_title: Andilly Art-Nature Trail · Land art · Family · Genevois
chemin-art-nature-andilly-andilly.meta_description: Family land-art trail at Andilly (Pays de Genève): wooden sculptures, Bougerie pond, 3 picnic tables, Erraticaborne shelter. Free access.
chemin-art-nature-andilly-andilly.hero_alt: Andilly Art-Nature trail in the Genevois with land art, wooden sculptures and Bougerie pond shelter
chemin-art-nature-andilly-andilly.body.what_is:
The Andilly Art-Nature Trail is a shaded path unlike any other: it invites each walker to become a land-art artist, creating and leaving along the trail an ephemeral or more lasting work made from what nature offers around (stones, wood, moss, earth). Along the way, walkers also discover genuine wooden sculptures installed by artists — a dialogue between permanent art and visitors' spontaneous creations.

The trail runs through the commune of Andilly (74350), in the Monts du Genevois, halfway between Saint-Julien-en-Genevois and Cruseilles. It is short and flat, accessible to children and occasional walkers alike, in shaded woodland ideal in summer. At the heart of the trail, a stop is offered by the Bougerie pond, where three picnic tables are available — perfect for a half-day family outing.

A small shelter ("Erraticaborne") provides cover in case of rain. Free access year-round.

debaroule-clusaz.name: Débaroule — Playful Trail
debaroule-clusaz.meta_title: Débaroule · La Clusaz · 7 modules · Playful summer trail
debaroule-clusaz.meta_description: Playful 7-module wooden-ball trail at La Clusaz: from the Patinoire cable car top down to the Bossonnet woods. Mountain summer.
debaroule-clusaz.hero_alt: Débaroule playful trail at La Clusaz with 7 wooden-ball modules through the alpine forest
debaroule-clusaz.body.what_is:
The Débaroule is a playful high-altitude trail laid out on the La Clusaz ski domain, accessible in summer from the top of the Patinoire cable car. Its twist: walkers descend on foot to the Bossonnet, accompanying their own wooden ball along the way through 7 playful wooden modules that make the ball roll, drop and run — using mechanical principles reminiscent of giant marble runs (wooden flippers, ramps, turns, accelerators, launches).

The experience is designed for families, suitable for children from 3–4 with an adult. The trail is easy and downhill, with no technical difficulty. The route is part of La Clusaz's summer activity package and the "Pass Découverte" combining foot-passenger lifts and several summer activities.

At the end of the Débaroule, children can carry on to the nearby Luge des Bois at Bossonnet. Summer season (July–August + weekends).

ecomusee-lac-annecy-sevrier.name: Lake Annecy Ecomuseum
ecomusee-lac-annecy-sevrier.meta_title: Lake Annecy Ecomuseum · Sevrier · €7 adult / €5 child
ecomusee-lac-annecy-sevrier.meta_description: Immersive museum at Sevrier on 19th-century Savoyard life by Lake Annecy: playful trails, puzzle-games for families. Adult €7, family €22.
ecomusee-lac-annecy-sevrier.hero_alt: Lake Annecy Ecomuseum at Sevrier, immersive exhibition on 19th-century Savoyard daily life by the lake
ecomusee-lac-annecy-sevrier.body.what_is:
The Lake Annecy Ecomuseum is one of the pivot museums of Lake Annecy: located at Sevrier (74320), on the western shore famous for its Paccard bell foundry, it has offered for several decades an immersive encounter with 19th-century Savoyards. The route explores daily life, crafts, traditions and landscapes of the Annecy basin at a pivotal time — just before the rise of thermal tourism and industrialisation.

The museum has heavily modernised its offer in recent years with a strong family focus: playful age-graded trails (2–3, 3–6 years), puzzle-games (6–10, 10+), and even an open-air riddle circuit available at the ticket office. The idea: let visitors live Savoyard daily sensations — handle, observe, guess — rather than simply look.

Adult €7, child €5, family pass €22. Open year-round (closed Tuesday and certain holidays). Parking and refreshment area on site.

ferme-ecomusee-clos-parchet-samoens.name: Clos Parchet Farm Ecomuseum
ferme-ecomusee-clos-parchet-samoens.meta_title: Clos Parchet Farm Ecomuseum · Samoëns · 1815 farm
ferme-ecomusee-clos-parchet-samoens.meta_description: Traditional 1815 farm turned ecomuseum at Samoëns: 2,000+ utensils, cuzna, pèle, byre, bread oven. Adult €8, under 12 free.
ferme-ecomusee-clos-parchet-samoens.hero_alt: Clos Parchet Farm Ecomuseum at Samoëns, traditional 1815 farm with collection of rural utensils
ferme-ecomusee-clos-parchet-samoens.body.what_is:
The Clos Parchet Farm Ecomuseum is one of the finest showcases of the Haut-Giffre rural heritage. A traditional 1815 farm carefully preserved and opened to the public in 1994, it brings back daily life on the sunny southern slopes of Samoëns. The collection includes over 2,000 period utensils — tools, cookware, looms, clogs, sledges — telling the story of farm work and the old trades of the upper Giffre valley.

Visits are guided by the Heritage Guides of Savoie Mont Blanc, through the cuzna (kitchen), pèle (parlour), byre, bread oven and barn. Adult €8, under 12 free; reservation recommended in season.

grand-casino-annemasse-annemasse.name: Grand Casino of Annemasse
grand-casino-annemasse-annemasse.meta_description: Partouche Grand Casino at Annemasse, near Geneva: 190 slot machines, Roulette, Blackjack, Ultimate Poker, Craps, poker room, Le Trèfle restaurant.
grand-casino-annemasse-annemasse.hero_alt: Grand Casino Partouche in Annemasse near Geneva, slot floor and traditional table-game lounge
grand-casino-annemasse-annemasse.body.what_is:
The Grand Casino of Annemasse is the Partouche Group gaming venue at the heart of Annemasse, at 2 avenue de l'Europe, a few minutes by car or tram from central Geneva. One of the two main casinos in Haute-Savoie on the Geneva side (with Saint-Julien-en-Genevois), it draws a local and Swiss-border clientele on evenings and weekends.

Games: 190 slot machines (video poker, classic and video reels) and a traditional table lounge with English Roulette, Blackjack, Ultimate Poker, Craps, plus a dedicated poker room. The Le Trèfle restaurant rounds out the evening. ID required (18+, no exclusion order).

itineraire-pedestre-bords-arve-gaillard-.name: Walking route: Banks of the Arve – Gaillard to Arthaz
itineraire-pedestre-bords-arve-gaillard-.meta_title: Banks of the Arve · Gaillard → Arthaz · 8.9 km trail (74)
itineraire-pedestre-bords-arve-gaillard-.meta_description: Walking and mountain-bike trail along the Arve between Gaillard and Arthaz-Pont-Notre-Dame: 8.9 km, 2 h, free access, views of Le Salève and Le Môle.
itineraire-pedestre-bords-arve-gaillard-.hero_alt: Banks of the Arve trail between Gaillard and Arthaz in Haute-Savoie, linear 8.9 km path
itineraire-pedestre-bords-arve-gaillard-.body.what_is:
The banks of the Arve path between Gaillard and Arthaz-Pont-Notre-Dame is a linear 8.9 km route laid out by the SM3A (Arve River Authority) as part of the major "Arve River Path from Léman to Mont-Blanc" project. Start at the Chenevières car park in Gaillard, finish at the Pont Neuf in Arthaz. About 2 hours at a relaxed walking pace, or 1 hour by bike.

The route alternates wooded natural stretches, urban sections (Étrembières, the Jardin des Iris, the Grand Casino) and farmland. Wide views of Le Salève and Le Môle. Free, year-round, suitable for families and bikes.

itineraire-pedestre-chemin-bords-dranse-.name: Walking route – Banks of the Dranse path
itineraire-pedestre-chemin-bords-dranse-.meta_title: Banks of the Dranse path · Châtel → Abondance · 16 km (74)
itineraire-pedestre-chemin-bords-dranse-.meta_description: Easy flat 16 km path along the Dranse between Châtel and Abondance: family-friendly, stroller, bike. Abondance valley, free.
itineraire-pedestre-chemin-bords-dranse-.hero_alt: Banks of the Dranse path between Châtel and Abondance in the Haut-Chablais, flat valley trail
itineraire-pedestre-chemin-bords-dranse-.body.what_is:
The Banks of the Dranse path is a walking trail and greenway between Châtel and Abondance, at the heart of the Abondance valley in the Haut-Chablais. The route follows the Dranse de Sous-le-Pas for about 16 km linear (up to 20 km in some variants), between the Très-les-Pierres hamlet on the Châtel side and the village centre of Abondance.

Flat and easy, accessible to all: families with toddlers, strollers, hikers, seniors, sporty walkers. The stabilised surface also suits MTB, hybrid and e-bikes. Free year-round.

jardins-europe-annecy.name: Jardins de l'Europe
jardins-europe-annecy.meta_description: On the shore of Lake Annecy, the Jardins de l'Europe form an English-style arboretum laid out in 1863: 250 remarkable trees, free 24/7 access.
jardins-europe-annecy.hero_alt: Jardins de l'Europe at Annecy on the lakeside, English-style arboretum with rare trees
jardins-europe-annecy.body.what_is:
The Jardins de l'Europe are the green lung of Annecy city centre: a 1.8 ha park-arboretum laid out between the Vassé canal, quai Eustache-Chappuis and the northern shore of Lake Annecy. Right next to the Pâquier and the Pont des Amours, this English-style garden now counts close to 250 trees including remarkable species: ginkgo biloba, tulip tree, Corsican pines, giant sequoias, plus more than 1,000 shrubs.

The site is rich in history: a marshland island in the Middle Ages, the "Clos Lombard" was given to the Visitation order in 1644 by a Lombard banking family, became national property at the Revolution, then bought back by the town between 1835 and 1843. Free 24/7.

maison-fromage-abondance-abondance.name: Abondance Cheese House
maison-fromage-abondance-abondance.meta_title: Abondance Cheese House · Cheese AOP · 2026 prices
maison-fromage-abondance-abondance.meta_description: Museum of AOP Abondance cheese: sensory route from alpine pastures to ripening cellars, tasting included. €5.50 adult, DUO Abbey ticket €9.
maison-fromage-abondance-abondance.hero_alt: Abondance Cheese House at Abondance, sensory route on the AOP cheese with tasting and ripening cellars
maison-fromage-abondance-abondance.body.what_is:
The Abondance Cheese House is the official interpretation museum of the AOP Abondance cheese, in the heart of the Abondance village in the Haut-Chablais. Run by the Abondance Patrimoine association, it offers a sensory scenographic route covering the full cheese cycle: from summer alpine pastures to ripening cellars, via milking, rennet, pressing and wheel ageing.

The visit highlights the patient gestures of breeders, cheesemakers and master ripeners of the Abondance Valley through images, sounds, hands-on stations and reconstructions. Visitors discover the local Abondance cow (brown-ringed eyes), her rich milk and the cheese's signature. Tasting included. Adult €5.50; DUO ticket with Abbey €9.

mine-lappiaz-morzine.name: The Lappiaz Mine
mine-lappiaz-morzine.meta_description: Slate interpretive trail at Morzine — former Lappiaz mine worked 1856-1963 at Avoriaz. Orientation table, free access.
mine-lappiaz-morzine.hero_alt: Former Lappiaz slate mine above Avoriaz at Morzine in Haute-Savoie, interpretive trail
mine-lappiaz-morzine.body.what_is:
The Lappiaz mine is a former slate quarry above the Pas de Chavanette, on the heights of Avoriaz (commune of Morzine). One of the main industrial remnants of the Haut-Chablais, it witnesses the extraction of "Grey Gold" — the Morzine slate — which marked the local economy for more than two centuries. The cliff above the Sous-le-Saix valley (known as the "Slate Quarry Valley") was worked from 1732; at Lappiaz itself, the mine operated from 1856 to 1963.

The site is now laid out as an interpretive trail signposted from the Avoriaz tourist office, passing between the Malinka and Saskia buildings down to the old works. Orientation table; free year-round access.

musee-ailes-anciennes-excenevex.name: Museum of Antique Wings
musee-ailes-anciennes-excenevex.meta_title: Museum of Antique Wings Excenevex · Aircraft · 2026 prices
musee-ailes-anciennes-excenevex.meta_description: Aviation association museum at Excenevex: aircraft, helicopters and gliders under restoration since 1992. Flight simulator, free access.
musee-ailes-anciennes-excenevex.hero_alt: Museum of Antique Wings at Excenevex, aircraft and helicopters under restoration on the Léman shore
musee-ailes-anciennes-excenevex.body.what_is:
The Museum of Antique Wings at Excenevex is run by the Ailes Anciennes Haute-Savoie association, created in 1992 and recognised for safeguarding France's aeronautical heritage. Located on the Sciez-Excenevex plain alongside Lake Geneva, it aims to become the leading aeronautical heritage museum of Haute-Savoie.

The collection brings together a variety of aircraft, helicopters, gliders and aviation pieces under restoration or on display, including a recently acquired Dassault Mirage IIIE n°571. Volunteers — retired pilots, mechanics, engineers — restore the machines. Flight simulator available on visit days. Free access; donations welcome.

musee-art-et-folklore-regional-fessy.name: Regional Art and Folklore Museum
musee-art-et-folklore-regional-fessy.meta_title: Regional Art and Folklore Museum Fessy · Muséâm · 2026 prices
musee-art-et-folklore-regional-fessy.meta_description: Regional art and folklore museum at Fessy: 20,000 rural objects by Bernard Lacroix, in an 18th-c. farm. Summer Thursday guided tours, €5.
musee-art-et-folklore-regional-fessy.hero_alt: Regional Art and Folklore Museum at Fessy, 18th-c. farm with 20,000 rural objects from Bernard Lacroix's collection
musee-art-et-folklore-regional-fessy.body.what_is:
The Regional Art and Folklore Museum of Fessy, better known as the Muséâm or Bernard Lacroix Museum, sits in an old 18th-c. family farm originally built by the Carthusians of Bellevaux. It holds the largest collection of rural objects in Haute-Savoie: over 20,000 pieces gathered over a lifetime by Bernard Lacroix, son of the village and great collector of alpine ethnology.

Every aspect of pre-modern peasant life is represented: smithy, bakery with bread oven, woodworking shop, clog-maker's workshops, weaving tools, press, carpentry shop. Guided tours on Thursdays in summer, €5; €3 reduced.

musee-art-sacre-saint-gervais-bains.name: Museum of Sacred Art
musee-art-sacre-saint-gervais-bains.meta_title: Museum of Sacred Art · Saint-Nicolas-de-Véroce · Saint-Gervais (74)
musee-art-sacre-saint-gervais-bains.meta_description: Sacred art museum in the 18th-c. former presbytery of Saint-Nicolas-de-Véroce: baroque Savoyard sculptures, silverware and liturgical ornaments.
musee-art-sacre-saint-gervais-bains.hero_alt: Museum of Sacred Art at Saint-Nicolas-de-Véroce, baroque Savoyard sculptures in the former 18th-c. presbytery
musee-art-sacre-saint-gervais-bains.body.what_is:
The Museum of Sacred Art is at Saint-Nicolas-de-Véroce, a high-altitude hamlet (≈1,170 m) of Saint-Gervais-les-Bains in the Mont Blanc area. It occupies the 18th-c. former presbytery, next to the remarkable baroque Saint-Nicolas church (Historic Monument). Opened in December 2010, it highlights the baroque sacred-art treasure of the parish, one of the richest in the diocese of Annecy.

The full collection counts over 600 objects, mostly 17th-18th c.; about fifty are permanently shown: polychrome wood sculptures (saints, Virgins, angels), silverware (chalices, ciboria), and liturgical ornaments. Reduced price; opening hours vary with the season.

musee-cinema-philippe-piccot-douvaine.name: Philippe Piccot Cinema Museum
musee-cinema-philippe-piccot-douvaine.meta_title: Philippe Piccot Cinema Museum · Douvaine · Film projectors
musee-cinema-philippe-piccot-douvaine.meta_description: Collection of Buisse-Bottazzi film projectors (1946-2007) at Douvaine. Philippe Piccot Museum open every Tuesday in July-August, 2:30-5:30 pm.
musee-cinema-philippe-piccot-douvaine.hero_alt: Philippe Piccot Cinema Museum at Douvaine, collection of Buisse-Bottazzi film projectors from 1946 to 2007
musee-cinema-philippe-piccot-douvaine.body.what_is:
The Philippe Piccot Cinema Museum at Douvaine is one of the most singular museums in Haute-Savoie: it gathers in the centre of Douvaine the complete collection of film projectors designed, built and sold by Jean Buisse and Paul Bottazzi, two Lyon inventors nicknamed the "Rolls-Royces of cinema projection". Their machines, produced between 1946 and 2007, equipped the finest cinemas across France and Europe.

The museum is the life's work of Philippe Piccot, who ran the Cinéma du Chablais in Douvaine and saved and restored every Buisse-Bottazzi model: projection-cabin machines, lab equipment and accessories. Open every Tuesday in July-August, 2:30-5:30 pm; free guided tours.

musee-cordonnerie-alby-sur-cheran.name: Shoemaking Museum
musee-cordonnerie-alby-sur-cheran.meta_description: In the heart of Alby-sur-Chéran's medieval village, the Shoemaking Museum traces 300 years of cobblers' tradition. Free entry, open June-August.
musee-cordonnerie-alby-sur-cheran.hero_alt: Shoemaking Museum at Alby-sur-Chéran in the medieval village arcades, 300 years of cobbler tradition
musee-cordonnerie-alby-sur-cheran.body.what_is:
The Shoemaking Museum of Alby-sur-Chéran is a small craft museum in the heart of the medieval village, at 9 place du Trophée, under the historic arcades where the village cobblers once worked. Created in 1980, it moved into the medieval village in 1999, unveiling the artisan past that shaped the village identity for centuries.

In the 19th c. Alby-sur-Chéran was a major leather centre in Savoy: about 300 cobblers in 1880 made the famous "Savoyard shoes" under the Trophée arcades. The museum reconstructs workshops, tools, and the whole local cobbler economy. Free entry; open in summer (June-August).

musee-departemental-sapeurs-pompiers-hau.name: Haute-Savoie Departmental Firefighters Museum
musee-departemental-sapeurs-pompiers-hau.meta_description: Haute-Savoie Firefighters Museum at Sciez: hand pumps, vehicles, uniforms 1900-2026. Adult €5, child €3.50. Domaine de Guidou.
musee-departemental-sapeurs-pompiers-hau.hero_alt: Haute-Savoie Departmental Firefighters Museum at the Domaine de Guidou in Sciez, on the Léman shore
musee-departemental-sapeurs-pompiers-hau.body.what_is:
The Haute-Savoie Departmental Firefighters Museum is housed at the Domaine de Guidou on the heights of Sciez, on Lake Geneva. Run by the Haute-Savoie Firefighters Union (UDSP 74) in partnership with the SDIS 74, it traces over a century of fire and rescue equipment and the rescuers who shaped the local memory.

The collection covers the 1900s to today: 19th-c. hand pumps, historic fire vehicles (pump trucks, tankers), period uniforms (copper helmets, jackets, breathing apparatus), extrication tools, extinguishers, hoses. Adult €5, child €3.50. Check opening days on the UDSP 74 website.

musee-ermitage-calvaire-megeve.name: Calvary Hermitage Museum
musee-ermitage-calvaire-megeve.meta_title: Calvary Hermitage Museum · Megève · Free entry (74)
musee-ermitage-calvaire-megeve.meta_description: Interpretation centre of Megève's Calvary (14 chapels 1840-1878, Historic Monument). Inaugurated 2013 at the Hermitage. Free entry; €5.50 guided tour.
musee-ermitage-calvaire-megeve.hero_alt: Calvary Hermitage Museum at Megève, interpretation centre for the 14 Calvary chapels from 1840-1878
musee-ermitage-calvaire-megeve.body.what_is:
The Calvary Hermitage Museum at Megève is the interpretation centre for Megève's Calvary, a major heritage site of the Mont Blanc area. Opened in 2013, it occupies the Hermitage, a former spiritual retreat, and tells the story of an exceptional project: the construction, between 1840 and 1878, of a Calvary path with 14 chapels and oratories retracing the Passion of Christ.

This path is the work of one man: Father Ambroise Martin (1791-1863), parish priest of Megève from 1820 to his death. Inspired by the Sacri Monti of Piedmont, he devoted more than forty years to seeing it through. Listed in the Supplementary Inventory of Historic Monuments. Free entry; guided tour €5.50.

musee-faune-bellevaux.name: Museum of Fauna
musee-faune-bellevaux.meta_title: Museum of Fauna Bellevaux · 200 mountain animals (74)
musee-faune-bellevaux.meta_description: Museum of Fauna at Bellevaux: 200+ naturalised mountain animals in reconstructed habitats, audio interactive stations. Adult €4, child €3.
musee-faune-bellevaux.hero_alt: Museum of Fauna at Bellevaux, more than 200 naturalised mountain animals in reconstructed alpine habitats
musee-faune-bellevaux.body.what_is:
The Museum of Fauna is one of the two Bellevaux museums, in this heritage village of the Brevon valley (Haut-Chablais). Under the town hall on the village square, it presents over 200 naturalised animals representative of the Haute-Savoie mountain fauna: chamois, ibex, marmots, foxes, lynxes, stoats, squirrels, black grouse, ptarmigans, golden eagles, bearded vultures, plus the main forest and alpine-pasture birds.

The animals are staged in reconstructed natural habitats — forests, alpine pastures, scree, mountain lakes — that bring them to life. Interactive sound stations teach visitors to recognise the calls and songs of the mountains. Adult €4, child €3.

musee-granges-servette-chens-sur-leman.name: Granges de Servette Museum
musee-granges-servette-chens-sur-leman.meta_title: Granges de Servette Museum · Chens-sur-Léman · Art (74)
musee-granges-servette-chens-sur-leman.meta_description: Granges de Servette Museum: contemporary art (Lyon school) and 1,000+ old tools at Chens-sur-Léman. Free entry, summer 3-7 pm.
musee-granges-servette-chens-sur-leman.hero_alt: Granges de Servette Museum at Chens-sur-Léman, contemporary art and rural tools in restored barns
musee-granges-servette-chens-sur-leman.body.what_is:
The Granges de Servette Museum is a private museum of contemporary art and rural heritage in restored old barns at the Collongette hamlet, commune of Chens-sur-Léman, just steps from Lake Geneva and the Swiss border. It has become a discreet but recognised cultural site of the Bas-Chablais.

The permanent collection, built up since 1958, gathers contemporary paintings and sculptures where the Lyon school (Cottavoz, Fusaro, Truphémus, Carzou…) is particularly well represented, alongside local Léman artists. Every summer the museum hosts temporary exhibitions for new artists, keeping it a lively creation hub. A second wing displays over 1,000 old tools. Free entry, summer 3-7 pm.
